p.seg: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, Patrick of Ryedale sends greeting.

Let present and future know me to have licensed and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place for my soul and the souls of all my ancestors and successors the donation of Robert of Barnoldby in respect of twenty acres of land, namely ‘Raven’s Fen’, which he made to the said church in free alms from the two ploughgates of land that Robert himself and his heirs hold of me and my heirs in the territory of Whitton which were given to him in return for his service in feu and heritage. Wherefore I will that the said monks should hold and possess the said twenty acres by the same boundaries which are contained in Robert’s own charter which the monks have concerning the same land, with all its liberties and pertinents, as freely, quitely, fully and honourably as Robert’s own charter bears witness and affirms, and just as they freely and quitely hold and possess any other alms. Saving my service which Robert himself and his heirs shall do for me and my heirs in return for the same land.
